reviews.review_text:
Man, I thought this was the game for me.  I love fishing sims, I love horror games, so this felt like it would be a winner.  Unfortunately, it just misses the mark.  

The horror elements are so vaguely there that the game feels more like a "dark cozy game" than anything.  You can play the game without really ever engaging with any of it, especially once you get the first few upgrades.  I could vibe with that if the game play and progression were fun, but after early game it got to be pretty dull.  The core experience of customizing the boat and exploring new islands never really evolves to keep the game feeling fresh.  After hitting the volcano islands and seeing the same copy/paste mission structure I was pretty much done.  

It's not a bad game, it's just not for me and not exactly what I expected after hearing so many positive reviews of the game.

One of the best games I’ve played in the last couple of years. I’ve always liked fishing and cosmic horror, but never really considered putting then together. It works incredibly well.

The atmosphere is really well crafted, and especially in the beginning, I was genuinely creeped out when sunset started to roll in and I was still at sea. The frantic journey back to a port whilst the darkness is slowly engulfing you, and you start hearing strange noises around you in the water is absolutely priceless. Once you learn the mechanics, you get a few abilities and upgrades, and you start to feel more in control of each given situation. Not enough to be calm whilst sailing the seas, mind you, but slightly more at ease.

The DLCs were a bit disappointing, The Pale Reach introduced the icy biome and did rather well with that, despite the Narwahl being a bit of a letdown. The Iron Rig’s mechanics were interesting, but I felt that the oil spills ruined the visual appeal of the ecosystems. 

All in all, really memorable, absolutely recommending this game.

such a beautifully crafted game with haunting visuals. the storyline and atmosphere is ominous but somehow comforting and cozy at the same time. very cool sea creatures to discover (esp during the scary ass nights)

i love the ocean, so this got addicting real fast

soundtrack is genius

Dredge is one massive vibe. As you commit to it, it does reveal itself to be a little too formulaic for its own good, but there are still a few interesting aspects to its mechanics and story to uncover until pretty late.

I'm not a fan of single player games and I'm not a fan of horror games, but this game is a fantastic blend of dread and excitement in the face of the unknown.

The first DLC is amazing, the second, while fun and useful feels shoe-horned in a bit.

i could never fish in real life. i don't have the patience for it. that said, it's my *favorite* thing to do in video games and this is my favorite video game in which to fish. top tier. so fun. would buy again. would get amnesia just to play for the first time again, 100/10

Excellent little game that rides the line between relaxing/casual and engaging very well. Plenty of content for the price of entry, although it must be said that much of it essentially amounts to completing lists of fish caught. Upgrading your boat is fun at first and effectively 'gates' the game content without feeling overly forced, but towards the halfway point the upgrades start to feel a bit too effective and lose some of the moody atmosphere as you blitz along in what may as well be a speedboat. The storyline is decent, although probably a little weak for real horror/Cthulu type fans, it also is effectively only really told in bookend style at the very start and ending of the game, the entire middle is just a chunk of gameplay

The DLC's are worthy additions, in particular the Iron Rig fleshes out several 'missing' QoL features, both are intended to played throughout the game more or less from the start, not as add-on content for the end game.
♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, haven't even gotten through half the game and this is an amazing game. Perfect combination of survival and peaceful fishing, if you like Subnautica highly recommend.

I liked this game and the whole theme around it. It has several concepts that aren't fully realized, but still enough to deliver an interesting world and ideas.
The fishing and story were ok, and everything is pretty average overall, but still recommendable.

As an Indie game, it's a good product with small flaws and missed opportunities. That said, I want more from these devs in the future.
Absolutely adored the main game and the Iron Rig DLC. The other DLCs weren't worth it in the slightest, though.
A fantastic fishing game, and a brilliant display of games as art. If you're okay with horror and can immerse yourself in the world, you will find it hard not to be satisfied with this game.

Honestly one of my favorite games by a really long shot. I love playing it after an extremely stressful day and the music, art, and gameplay is simply legendary. I even dedicated an entire presentation around Dredge because this game has genuinely helped with my stress levels and allowed me to connect with people IRL over the love of this game.

All in all a fantastic game, I cannot recommend it enough!
Gameplay loop is very fun but it gets repetitive if you wanna go for completionist. Story is fun and the lore is nice. Overall a fun experience.
good game, very underated, just avoid buying pale reach dlc

I had a lot of fun playing this game. the tutorial is fast and easy to understand, the gameplay is enjoyable, and the storyline is able to quickly draw you in. my only 2 gripes with DREDGE is that there's only 5 main areas in the base game, making the game fly by. my other gripe is that the DLCs are almost the same price as the base game. Overall, if you want a fairly cheap, fun game, you enjoy fishing games, you want a subnautica-esque experience, or you think it looks cool, i would recommend playing DREDGE. 8.9/10
I hated the controls on Xbox, but this is a great game. Worth every penny I spent on it.

[h3]Fishing simulator in Mythos setting.[/h3]
Visuals are heavily stylized (but not pixel-art, thank God). Music and sounds are present.
Peculiar characters, quests, artifacts, and most of all creatures of the sea.
Whole encyclopedia of weird sea fauna for you to collect and a boat to improve!
Pure gameplay, no story worth mentioning and a straightforward ending (sounds harsh but I really like how it was executed).
Plays really nice and does not take itself too seriously. Reasonable playing time.

DLCs
Blackstone Key - Couple of semi-useful items. Not worth buying.
The Iron Rig - One new port, a bunch of new mechanics, creatures, upgrades and items. Your sea will never look pretty since then.
The Pale Reach - One brand new location. Nice addition to the rest of the world.

A boring Frustrating Chore. its incredibly Slow paced boring feels more like a chore then a fun experience i almost fell asleep playing this this game provides zero clear instructions and just throws u into the wasteland. I love fishing games but this one is a hard miss and pass. if u disagree find i guess its your taste good for u but i spent my money on it so i have a right to my opinion on it. i really wish i could refund this boring game

I do not recommend to 100% of it, the game can kind of get repetitive, and I do believe people go too easy on Indie games. Perhaps I am being to nit picky but the game's difficulty does not increase, the catching minigame does not change too much. Maybe it's for accessibility but it's just kind of a boring time throughout the game, the novelty at the start wears off about 1/4 into it.

The DLC does make the experience better overall but that shouldn't be the case.

The Lovecraftian stuff is kind of cool but is not utilized enough with the monsters being little to no danger half the time.

I just, I can't recommend it and also not recommend it.

Dredge has been on my radar for a good couple years, and I'm glad I finally got around to playing. It is [i]incredibly[/i] well-written. Like I think it'd be fun even if the writing was dogwater; the fishing gameplay loop is great, and I really enjoyed catching all the rares. With everything together, though, it's something of a masterpiece. Really looking forward to picking up the DLCs next time there's a sale.

It's a really well-rounded slow burn of a game, got everything you would expect from a fishing/ economy simulator with a couple spins on the formula. I'm especially a fan of the Tetris/ Resident Evil inventory and the surprising variety of fishing minigame formats.
I haven't finished the game, treating it as an in-between where I sit down with it for an hour or two a few times a week. Every session, it manages to leave me itching for the next inch of progression in both my equipment and information on the lore- and I think that's a good sign.
The graphics are heavily stylized and clean, with a really cool heavy-handed painter aesthetic that carries over well between the 2d and 3d assets. Its also always a joy to find new freaky fish types, with tons (hundreds?) of unique designs. On the horror side of things, DREDGE obviously leans Lovecraft but in a refreshingly reserved way. I wouldn't say the game is scary per se, moreso tense in atmosphere. Story implications, sanity, and the odd big hostile fish keep things spooky, but not to the point of taking away from the main gameplay loop. Gotta say though, the sense of scale is really well done, especially when you see something absolutely massive slither beneath your little fishing boat.
I got the complete edition, which from other reviews seemed like the best choice cause the individual DLC is so-so in total content. Together, though? Killer.
Good game. Check out the complete edition if you want more content.

What I Enjoyed:
Dredge seems simple on the outside—catch fish, sell fish, and repeat—but the entire game is much more mysterious and convoluting than that. Though the game follows a similar gameplay loop throughout a play through, the horror elements mixed in contrast with the peaceful fishing, alongside the beautiful and well-crafted map, keeps the game from feeling overly monotonous. The Music and sound design blend in perfectly with the feeling the game is trying instil—fear, suspense, and anticipation—enhancing the overall experience without feeling obtrusive. NPC's in the game all carry original and compelling dialogue, in which they gradually reveal the game's convoluting story. Even after exploring the entire map, with how the game obfuscates many things—such as pensive notes that leave you with more questions than answers or conversations with NPC's that help you get acquainted with the different islands' inhabitants. You can progress through the game at your own pace—usually without ever having to fear death—since there are many save points around the map; the game encourages exploration by making most of the play through stress-free. Just know that there will always be a looming sense of dread and tension hanging in the air as you traverse through the map, knowing that anything—Great White Sharks, Whales, and Orcas—could be under your boat.

What Could Be Improved:
Though the game encourages exploration, there are occasional moments where it can feel like your hitting a wall. Your progress suddenly halts and goes stagnant, leaving you wandering around the map without knowing was to do. This happened to be during my play through of the game, and it was REALLY demotivating and frankly annoying. Secondly, the horror aspect of the game—the monsters, not the map's atmosphere—is really lack laster. You will barely see most of the monsters that this game has to offer since their locked behind a sanity feature. This addition is cool, but it really decreases the fear factor of a play through, especially since reaching the lowest sanity—the point where monsters start spawning—will never really happen unless your actively trying to do so. Also, most monsters only feel frightening and horrific the first encounter but slowly become more of a nuisance as the game progresses. This problem is only exacerbated especially deeper into a play through when you get abilities and upgrades that allow you to completely outrun them. This is a hot take, but I honestly feel that the monsters designs are not inherently scary. They look cool, for sure, but not ghastly nor creepy. The game really tries to be a creepy fishing game with super natural monsters, but I just never really felt that in my play through.

Conclusion:
The game is a pretty relaxing fishing game with a wide range of fauna in an expansive map, providing you with many quests, NPC's to converse with, and a convoluting story that will leave you pondering even after completing the main story-line. Though the game lacks ghastly sea monsters, it makes up for that by crafting an eerie ambiance and atmosphere. Overall, a fun game that I would definitely recommend to anyone interest in an open world exploration-orientated fishing game.
Very fun and relaxing (except at night) would not recommend playing at 3 AM it is a good way to get your heart pounding. 10/10 would play again!
Good art, good story, good minigames, a good game.

this game is fabulous. it's a mixture of atmospheric exploration and slightly spooky weird stuff, plus a lot of sort of crafting-y things. like stardew valley meets 19th century nautical novel with a touch of lovecraftian horror. lots of quests large and small, trading, buying, selling, fishing, and, of course, dredging. a blast, certainly.

One of the best Singleplayergames I have played. If you like the mix of upgrading your ship to make your life easier and a lovecraftian story. Buy it. The Complete Edition! And do all Steam Achievements!
Fun to drive the boat around and catch fish! Love seeing the aberrations. Story is also pretty good. I really like the art style and the little additions of other non-catchable sea life!

This is a very creepy but fun game. The base game definitely gives you a good idea of choices at the end. The DLCs do  give more content and gameplay, but i have some reservations about it. I just completed The Pale Reach main story last night and after all the creepy lead up i thought there would be more. The “ending” kinda fell flat. I have more questions that i feel are unanswered. The Iron Rig like what happens to the scientist? Do we see him again? Is he going to cause havoc to us in the game? And the weird “oil” like spill. What else is there to do with that? And just it staying over in the Devils Spine, its just there now. Once you get all the upgrades you dont really need that weird oil spooky stuff. It feels very open ended and unless you wanna destroy your boat by going insane theres not much else to do. 
Overall though i enjoyed the game it hit the creepy factor and the go at your pace, just wish it closed the stories more or have them all tie into each other in some manner than just separate side quests. 

But go play it for yourself you might feel differently than i do.

This game's graphics style is perfect. The story is a 10/10 with dialogue making you get to understand the characters extremely well. The inventory is also very cool and unique making you have to place the fish like Tetris. When you catch a fish a mini game will pop up and you have to beat it to get the fish(different mini game for every type of fish). At different islands you can customize your boat anyway you would like. You can upgrade your boat with lights, fishing rods, paint, flags, motors. The BEST part has to be that its open world letting you control you're little boat on the huge open waters.

This Game is worth the money. I bought all the DLCs and they were just peak. This Game has such a great Soundtrack too. Its just that if you played it 100% once you wont come back. very good game tho :D

"And when Alexander saw the breadth of his domain, he wept, for there were no more worlds to conquer"

Good game, I wish there was more but I know that if there was it'd only spoil the meal. Also game ran fine though it did have some trouble with the undocking when holding down X, crashed once or twice, safer to just click undock.

You're a fisherman whose washed up on a little town. You fish in exchange for money to be used to upgrade your boat and equipment. A cool narrative plays out took keep you curious and engaged. Artistically well-done. The game has a defined aesthetic and it's soundtrack suits it perfectly. A quaint, interesting, simple game that was surprisingly interesting and fun.
Great art, storytelling and just the right amount of horror.

A pretty good time, even if the last 1/3 tends to drag and the ending is fairly shallow (pun intended). I had a decent enough ~15 hours here, but I probably won't ever 100% the game, buy any of the DLCs, or replay it. 

I enjoyed this game for its atmosphere, sound design, and above all else, descriptions. The creepy descriptions on all the aberrant fish alone are worth the price of admission. 

But when you get right down to it, the game never has *quite* enough actual game mechanics to hold my interest.

I’ve unlocked 100% of the achievements. 🏆
To me, this is a surprisingly cozy game, even with its horror theme and the occasional monster chase. The core gameplay loop focuses heavily on grinding for resources to upgrade your boat, fishing gear, and equipment. It’s incredibly fun to just explore the waters, fill out your encyclopedia, and hunt down all the different fish species alongside their aberration variants. Highly recommended!
A really enjoyable game with good core mechanics and strong visuals. Only letdown is disappointing ending

A cozy horror game for the small population of people who actually enjoyed stardew valley's fishing minigame (me)

Runs great on my low end pc :) If you're bad with the horror genre then there's ways to avoid the horrors that you can unlock that don't feel like cheating imo. The game also feels great to 100%!! the achievements are rewarding, and any grinding you have to do requires skill and thought, not just spamming the same action over and over again.

10/10 game that is on my Mt. Rushmore  of games. It’s just a well designed game, relaxing(during the day), and the music is great too. Now that I’m finished with the game, I still listen to the soundtrack on Spotify while I’m at work.
Had a ton of fun playing this game, even 100%ed all Steam achievements. It scratched that "management/item collection game" itch perfectly, cool spooky vibes. Highly recommend!

I love fishing. This game and its art is beautiful. The vast ocean of secrets and terror is an amazing experience. I wont spoil much since this game is one that you need to experience to feel it. Would recommend playing.

You know, as a gamer, most of the times you learn that in game when you see something in the distance you always want to reach it and collect all, etc. well, Dredge is a game where you actually see stuff and you are like "NOP! Not going there, no thanks!"

Jokes aside, insane game! Beautiful art, amazing sound and vibe, the eerie feelings make you unsure about going out at night or night or at all, ever, again. The mechanics and gameplay is really nice aswell, everything is really nicely put together.

If you like eerie games with a little twist, this is a game for you. 

p.s. You'll probably start avoiding water from this point on. :D

p.s. Hire me please, I'd like to work on a game like this (dev here :D)
Very well-made game and super cool art style. Definitely recommend!

I think the mark of a truly great game (and this applies to all media and art) can be that once you finish it you're already thinking about starting it again.

At the time of writing I finished Dredge a few weeks ago and I can't get it out of my head. A brilliant balance between relaxing fishing sim and nerve-shredding horror adventure with a compelling story and superb, accessible gameplay. I'll see you again soon, I'm sure.

I love this game!
During the day, it is a calm fishing/exploration game. 
At night the fog rolls in, and the fog hides things that stalk in the dark...

Game play is simple and engaging to grasp. I love the slow build of the story.

I am very much a completionist, I must find and catch every one of these fish.
And I want to see all the monsters too.
Immersive gameplay, good music. DLC adds a lot of content and is well worth.

LOVED this game.
The atmosphere, the vibes, the story, the gameplay. It's all there. 
Chill fishing simulator? yes
Weird lovecraftian horror? yes
It's casual, relaxing but also worrying.
I wasn't as much a fan of the DLCs but theyre still fine, and the game stands on its own very well.
Truly, a gem.
solid. buy it with dlc since its better to progress it at the same time. also great on steamdeck

The Sea is Calling, and It Has Too Many Eyes.

DREDGE is what happens when Animal Crossing goes out past its bedtime and meets H.P. Lovecraft. By day, it's an incredibly relaxing, borderline therapeutic loop of sailing around, catching exotic fish, organizing your inventory like a tiny game of Tetris, and slowly upgrading your boat. 

By night... you realize the ocean is a cold, dark place that wants you dead. 

The Good:
* Beautiful, painterly art style that somehow makes existential dread look gorgeous.
* Highly addictive gameplay loop ("Just one more day of fishing...").
* Brilliant panic mechanic that makes you hallucinate when the fog rolls in.
* Inventory management that is oddly satisfying.

The Bad:
The day/night cycle passes so fast you'll constantly be screaming at the sunset.

Come for the cozy fishing, stay because a giant, glowing aberration mutated your squid and a massive sea monster is currently tearing your hull to shreds. 

rating 7/10
very fun and relaxing but also stressful and scary.

I thought this was a chill fishing game where i could relax, at some parts it was but my first time seeing the fake boat  changed my mind. I love this game and its been one of my fav games from the start the 100% the game, Highly recommended for anyone looking for a chill game with somhorror elements. The story is also great with the secret ending and the plot twists

Dredge is a very niche kind of horror game.  The world building is spectacular and feels real. It gives me the same feelings and vibes I got visiting family in Rhode Island when I was a child. That old, cold, oceanic you get mixed with the dark history and tales of off shore monsters. The far north volatile and cold ocean. Not some tropical paradise in the Gulf of Mexico. Deep, dark, and cold coasts off Northeastern USA and Canada. Fisherman with haunting tales of sighting lovecraftian monsters while trying to make a living. You are a seemingly simple fisherman working to repair and upgrade your vessel. Completing small jobs for weary looking locals. However you soon discover something more sinister is going on within the depths of the beautiful and chaotic ocean. I had not watch any youtube videos or consumed any content relate to this game before I made my purchase. Dredge does not disappoint. If you like exploration, lore, inventory management, and horror this game is perfect. Not to mention the absolutely gorgeous and fitting low poly style of graphics. This game is an addictive and unsettling masterpiece. BTW this game runs flawlessly on this pc (specs should be added to my review) and my steam deck. A truly versatile gaming experience. 10/10

Cute short game. Great atmosphere. Each zone had its own thing, which was nice.

The later upgrades are kind of meaningless and I was pretty oblivious to the book system.

The panic system was cute, though barely had any effect except for manifesting obstacles, though maybe I was just too careful to get to the good stuff.

I am obsessed with this game! This is THE fishing game to beat all fishing games!

I love that there is plenty of accessibility features if certain parts of the game are more difficult for you or if things get too repetitive for your liking. There are things like passive mode which makes the horror elements way more easy to handle if you are not great at handling stressful situations with big monsters. As well as the minigames having an auto complete feature if the timing is difficult to get down. I used a combination of these features at different times and it made the game so much more enjoyable! It definitely helped a bunch on my second playthrough so that I wouldn't be too bogged down with the gameplay and I could focus on the story and achievements hunting.
Very awesome game, super unique style and great light little story. One DLC is okay and the other is amazing. 9/10

The fishing part of this game is good.
The eldritch horror makes it great or at least that is what the eyes of the sea are telling me.
10/10

Dredge is just straight‑up fun. The fishing loop feels good, upgrades come at a nice pace, and it never turns into a grind. The atmosphere is the real hook — chill during the day, “okay something is definitely watching me” at night.

The story and mystery stay interesting without dragging things out, and the game respects your time, which I appreciate as someone who likes getting achievements without needing a second job.

If you want a relaxing game with just enough weird ocean horror to keep you awake, Dredge is absolutely worth it. 9.5/10 — would fish in cursed waters again. Definitely something fun to enjoy while listening to a podcast.

I've finished the base game and it's quite fun to play. The story and storytelling are decent, though you'll need to look things up online to fully understand the lore. The horror elements are good at the start, but by the mid-game, they become pretty average. Overall, it's worth the money whether you buy it at full price or on sale. As for the DLCs, I plan to finish all of them in the future.
great game, its a fun time to sail the seas, fish and run from the horrors of the deep.
Chill game with a nice vibe. Little short, but worth it.
I love the open world aspect of it and the challenges it provides. The art design and the collection design is very addicting and appealing.
A bit formulaic, but still a great game. It has a unique atmosphere that somehow blends comfy/cozy vibes with lovecraftian horror, which is always a plus in my book ;)

Delightful, absorbing game. Quaint villages. Interesting storylines. Lots to do. At night there are attacks by tentacled monsters and self-inflicted paranoia. By day there are picturesque islands to explore and sometimes whales and dolphins will emerge from the water.

I really enjoyed this game! Contains a great story, great quest line (with fun side quests too), and two worthwhile DLCs! I waited to buy this game until both DLCs were out, and I do not regret that decision at all. While the base game is already very rewarding and gives the player plenty to do, the DLCs were truly icing on the cake for me. Something I wish I had known going into the base game is that the DLCs are doable around mid base game. 

NOTE FOR NEW PLAYERS: do not be afraid of waiting to do the DLCs!! They can seriously help out in late base game and also allow for a small break from the main questline.
very deep story line, super addicting, 10/10 would recommend
Honestly a really solid cozy fishing game. Although it has horror elements it can be kinda chill. I love this game and it has so much replayability.

Honestly really great. the story is amazing and so is the artstyle and the gameplay is nice and relaxing while still not being a snoozefest and also that element of horror! absolutely recommend getting this game its amazing!

Cool game. Nice loop with a bit of risk involved. If getting with DLC and care for an suggestion:

Play Ice area in or around the 2nd-3rd story areas. Then do Iron Rig in conjunction with the last two areas.

I don't think I've ever seen a game that has such deep storytelling other than this, so much of it can be completely missed just if you have no intention of exploring, all the hidden secrets make exploration feel that much more ominous. Though I personally never felt afraid playing this game, it felt comforting in that weird spooky way, like every fish I reeled up was a reward for courage. Night fishing was my favorite. I adored when I reeled up something that didn't seem quite right. The way to reel up fish was so unique too, I loved how it changed with different fish. 10/10 loved completing this game
Nice game, a little short but otherwise worth it.
Good balance between chill fishing and nervous energy when the sun goes down and the fog rolls in. Really liked the game and story
A really fun and cozy in a way game to play, I've enjoyed this game and highly recommend it.

I really enjoyed this game. Playing this for the first time was such a unique and special experience. The world, the hidden mysteries, the music, and of course, the fishing is a captivating experience. Even just sailing your little boat around the islands is a blast. Like another reviewer said, I wish I could play this game for the first time again. It's so fun turning over every stone and finding every secret. I wish there was more. I really want a sequel. It would be cool if you could get new boats and some more cargo transport missions. I really loved this game, I think you might also.

I believe DREDGE is an objectively good game.

The art style: rocks. This game looks like a painting.
The music: rocks. The soundtrack is very well put together.

The gameplay loop: I want you to f#cking guess. It rocks. Fish up fish, sell fish, use fish money and ocean trash to upgrade your boat and catch much more ridiculous fish. Then go to a fun new biome and do it all over again!

Despite all these pros, I feel as though DREDGE isn't worth the sum of its parts, and it hurts me to say that, believe me!
I'd positively love to get molly whopped by a bus and isekai'd into DREDGE. At the same time, I would only be able to get *so* excited because I'd be aware of the fact that my fishing adventure ends with a twist that- spoiler- I've already seen in fiction many time before.

It's a good game! I love my little tug boat. The fishing is fun, and if you're looking for a game to immerse yourself in while you wait for your edible to kick in, DREDGE is a great purchase! Though if you're looking for a groundbreaking story you've never seen before, either look elsewhere, or wait for this game to go on sale and buy it anyway.


FINAL SCORE:

4 phantom rocks out of 5
"♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, that's what I get for speeding at night."

-Great atmosphere, with a unique aesthetic and interesting lore
-It is very fun to fish, dredge, and upgrade your boat.
-Some gameplay mechanics feel a little unfair or badly tuned, the day/night cycle feels a little fast
Very unique, creepy, Lovecraftian experience.

Fairly short game to 100% complete, but it's incredibly atmospheric and almost always engaging with the countless tasks you can choose to undertake. Even when grinding for the harder achievements, the in-game music is really enjoyable on its own. 

Discovering everything there is to the mechanics, world, characters, and horrors of this game is genuinely really fun, and the quick progression kept me from feeling bored or frustrated at all.

Solid 9/10 game 👍
It's a fun game but it's rather short, maybe ~15 hours worth of base gameplay (and another ~5hrs with the DLC) and I'm not sure if it's worth the experience

Honestly loved this game, such an engaging storyline and a gorgeous art style.

Got a good 15 hours for the main story so would probably prefer to buy on sale rather than full price if I were to do it again.

Noite escura, apenas balas assobiam pelo oceano
Apenas o vento zumbe nos fios, as estrelas brilham fracamente...
Simple, yet fun and strategic. I started before the 2 DLC's and really enjoyed the entire story line, and am looking forward to future content. Would definitely recommend!

I must have played a different game than everyone else because this was one of the most boring games I have played in the last decade. Its "horror" in the same way that Frankenstein or Dracula is horror, so take that for what you will. I personally found it very mundane, with the typical Lovecraftian tentacle based fish and not much else.

The fishing "mini-game" (and I hate to say minigame because it's actually 95% of the game, gets really old really quickly for the amount of times you need to do it. And the game is just way too slow, the upgrades are just stat upgrades, with hardly any unique gameplay changes so things again get stale quickly. it's trying to be a cozy fishing game and a horror game at the same time, which IMO just doesn't work. The tension just isn't built enough.
Wonderful little zen fishing game that has enough storytelling and atmosphere to make it feel worthwhile and more than the sum of its parts.

This is one of those games I wish I could forget just so that I could replay it again fresh. I absolutely love it. I've almost 100%ed it (barring 1 or 2 achievements) which I rarely do, but I just needed more of it! Even with the DLCs, I found myself wanting more. If you're looking for fresh content (not just more fishing) I think you'll have a hard time finding it after the 40 hour mark (including DLCs).

That said, I absolutely think it's worth getting this game anyway, as it's easily one of my favorite games of all time. The emergent storytelling in this game is wonderful and the character art is extremely evocative. The soundtrack is consistently beautiful and nerve-wracking. The FISHING, oh wow, the fishing. It's so much fun. I have a bit of an ocean fear and found some parts of this game pretty scary. I admit that after I got access to bait I rarely went out at night unless I had to (though when exploring at night after finishing everything else, I realized how many cool creature designs i had missed, so bear that in mind). I could have just turned the settings (yes, there are phobia and difficulty settings) to something safer, but I liked the thrill.

In any case, I just love DREDGE and it has created a craving in me that no other game has managed to fill since. Beware!

It's an okay game, the game loop itself is very fun but the story is a solid meh. 

Endgame felt very shallow and tedious at one point. Finally getting your boat fully upgraded only to have an hour or two left of meaningful story was a major disappointment for me.

 I know other won't feel as I do about it but that's the way it goes. I'd recommend buying it on sale, $24.99 for what is a "fishing" sim with glorified mini games makes the full price a hard recommendation.
Fun little fishing game for the steam deck. I can play casually and just fish and upgrade or I can go all out and try and survive the eldrich horrors.

Game's Aight. Has an interesting storyline. Be warned that if you want to 100% the game on steam you'll need the iron rig and the pale reach DLC. The fact that you need dlc for the 100% sucks. 8.5/10 would be 10 if steam achievements weren't locked behind DLC content.

This game was excellent. Its relatively simple appearance overlies well-done game mechanics and a spooky plot that almost never failed to hold my interest. My favorite part is when you catch freaky, deformed fish—the game's artists and writers worked really hard on developing myriad monstrosities. 

The way you catch fish, dredge up treasure and upgrade your boat are all intuitive and entertaining. I never felt lost, and I always felt like I had a variety of interesting side quests to try while I still continued advancing the main plot. 

The real fish species depicted (the non-deformed versions) are cool. So was the cast of human characters. You can tell that this game was created or influenced directly by people who work on, or study, marine life. From shipwrights to whalers and marine scientists, you see a diverse group of people who all add to the story. The character artwork for each person is unique, and a wide variety of races, ages and other traits are depicted.

This game is a fun way to unwind while enjoying some Edgar Allan Poe-esque themes and Lovecraftian creature elements. Absolutely try it anytime of year—especially around Halloween. The only downside is that it's for one player only, but it would still be fun for friends to watch.

One of my absolute favorite solo games. The vibe of the game being calm yet also turning unsettling in an instant is great. A takes a fairly simple mechanic and seems to flesh it out really well without being entirely repetitive or tedious. Would absolutely recommend to anyone thinking about purchasing.

if you don't like collection/ inventory organizing/exploration games for lacking combat action, this is an exception. I was hesitant to play it for that very reason, I'm not into this genre at all, but the almost entirely positive reception in combo with the aesthetic and atmosphere convinced me to give it a try. turns out they were right. this game is  somehow melancholy, serene, sorrowful, mysterious, unsettling and most importantly, fun. 

I guess the joke is on me, I'm the collector now

Que buena sorpresa este juego. Un juego indie, simple pero con un farmeo que funciona. Una exploración que uno termina deseando que tuviese un mapa mucho mayor porque realmente está bien logrado. En las misiones se puede percibir que es un indie al que le falta desarrollo pero no por eso deja de ser un juego más que recomendable.

I spent 60 hours on this game to 100% it and I wish I could purge this game from my mind to be able to play it for the first time again. This is probably one of my favorite games I've ever played. It's got fishing, horror, and has a touch of cozy when you aren't sailing for your life from the horrors below.
Very good sit back and relax fishing game, there is some level of tension during the nights but its pretty chill even then

Dredge is way better than it looks at first.

At the start it seems like a simple fishing game, but the more you play, the stranger it gets. The atmosphere is the best part. Sailing around during the day feels calm and relaxing, but once night hits, the game gets creepy fast.

The gameplay loop is really satisfying too. Catch fish, upgrade your boat, explore new areas, and slowly figure out what’s actually going on. It never feels too complicated, but it keeps giving you reasons to go back out for one more trip.

I also liked how the horror is handled. It’s not just cheap jump scares. It’s more of a weird, uneasy feeling that keeps building the longer you stay out at sea.

Overall, Dredge is a really good mix of relaxing and unsettling. It’s simple, addictive, and has a great atmosphere. Definitely worth playing if you want something different.

This is one of the best games ive played in my life all of the fishing mechanics are extremely fun and i would hand my credit card to team 17 because how how great they are the game thrives off of a building sense of fear from the big ass fish that want they booty the cethulu ahh story is also amazing and allows for tons of world buidling please buy its so good
Amazing game. interesting plot, interesting gameplay, great soundtrack, and beautiful visuals.
Very interesting game, Somehow peaceful and haunting at the same time.
Very chill and creepy lovecraftian fishing survival game

w fishing game
insane vibes 
gameplay is a bit, just a bit too repetitive and short
but the gameplay, worldbuilding and everything ties together really well

Wonderful artstyle, cozy feeling, fun inventory management, a looming sense of impending doom, loads of fish, easy to pause and come back to, good achievements, great music, interesting and diverse environments, a satisfying system for upgrades, and a fun storyline you can get through in a reasonable time even if you dont have the ability to sink a thousand hours into the game. The only thing i would change in this game is that I would add a wonderful ocean goddess to worship. This may be my favourite game of all time.
Excellent game  worth the price, short but worth it.
